ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT CYPRUS AMAX MINERALS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. FOR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PURPOSES, THERE IS NO REQUIREMENT THAT A COMPLAINT ALLEGE THAT A DEFENDANT IS SUED IN A PARTICULAR CAPACITY, WHETHER INDIVIDUAL, AS A SUCCESSOR, BOTH OR OTHERWISE, AT LEAST WHERE, AS HERE, THE AMENDMENT NAMING DEFENDANT DID NOT DISCLAIM ANY PARTICULAR CAPACITY. THUS, THE AMENDMENT NAMING DEFENDANT IN PLACE OF A DOE DEFENDANT IS THE RELEVANT PLEADING FOR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PURPOSES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. IN ADDITION, THE COURT SUSTAINED DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFFS SUR-REPLY. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077. (220)

MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jun-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CYPRUS AMAX MINERALS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

APPEARANCES: Stephen M. Tigerman, Esq. of Harowitz & Tigerman, LLP 788-1588 for pltfs & opposing parties; Anthony Bentivegna, Esq. & Lysle Kapp, Esq. of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer 510/658-3600 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. For statute of limitations purposes, there is no requirement that a complaint allege that a defendant is sued in a particular capacity, whether individual, as a successor, both or otherwise, at least where, as here, the amendment naming defendant did not disclaim any particular capacity. Thus, the amendment naming defendant in place of a DOE defendant is the relevant pleading for statute of limitations purposes. Order signed in open court. In addition, the Court sustained defendant's objection to plaintiffs sur-reply. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(220/HEK)
